---
title: "MayWASOAnalysis"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Coding Using Master File Data

# Spaghetti Plots

```{r}
#Master File Code 
hd_ids   <- c(2, 4, 9, 3, 14, 18, 22, 23, 25, 26, 29, 30, 31, 35, 42) # Isolate Huntington's Disease group: participants 2, 3, 4, 9, 14, 18, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27(NO DATA REMOVE), 29, 30, 31, 35, 37(not enough data in this file, so not included), 42
ctrl_ids <- c(19, 20, 21, 41, 43)  # Isolate Control group: participants 19, 20, 21, 40(not here due to not enough days), 41, 43, (45+47 lack data in this file)

# cleaning function, filter out participants with under 600 minute Weartime and days where WASO is NA or blank in the master sheet
clean_data <- function(df, ids, group_label = NULL) {
  df %>%
    rename(
      SUBJECT   = Participant.ID,
      STAGE     = Stage,
      NIGHT     = Night,
      WEAR_TIME = Wear.Time,
      WASO      = WASO.minutes
    ) %>%
    filter(SUBJECT %in% ids) %>%
    mutate(
      STAGE = case_when(
        STAGE %in% c("Mild Stage 3", "Moderate Stage 3") ~ "Stage 3",
        STAGE == "Control"                                ~ "Control",
        TRUE                                              ~ STAGE
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(WEAR_TIME >= 600) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(WASO), !is.na(NIGHT)) %>%
    filter(NIGHT >= 1 & NIGHT <= 15) %>%
    mutate(SUBJECT = as.factor(SUBJECT))
}

raw <- read.csv("Master_Data_05_07_26.xlsx.csv")

waso_data <- clean_data(raw, hd_ids)
ctrl_data <- clean_data(raw, ctrl_ids)


# HD Spaghetti Plot Code, make each participant line grey and overlay an average line for each HD stage
stage_colors <- c("Stage 2" = "navy", "Stage 3" = "red")
stage_shapes <- c("Stage 2" = 16,        "Stage 3" = 17)

ggplot(waso_data, aes(x = NIGHT, y = WASO, group = SUBJECT)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(color = "grey70", size = 1.8, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = STAGE, color = STAGE),
    fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.8, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = STAGE, color = STAGE, shape = STAGE),
    fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = stage_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = stage_shapes) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 15), breaks = 1:15) +
  labs(
    title = "HD WASO Time Trends Over 2 Weeks By Participant",
    x = "Night", y = "WASO (minutes)", color = "HD Stage", shape = "HD Stage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.line          = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8),
    axis.ticks         = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.background   = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing      = unit(1, "lines"),
    panel.border       = element_rect(color = "grey40", fill = NA),
    text               = element_text(size = 15),
    plot.title         = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position    = "right"
  )


# Repeat for Control Spaghetti Plot 
ggplot(ctrl_data, aes(x = NIGHT, y = WASO, group = SUBJECT)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(color = "grey70", size = 1.8, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean, geom = "line",
    color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1.8, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean, geom = "point",
    color = "darkgreen", size = 3.5, shape = 15
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, 15), breaks = 1:15) +
  labs(
    title = "Control WASO Time Trends Over 2 Weeks By Participant",
    x = "Night", y = "WASO (minutes)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.line          = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8),
    axis.ticks         = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.background   = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing      = unit(1, "lines"),
    panel.border       = element_rect(color = "grey40", fill = NA),
    text               = element_text(size = 15),
    plot.title         = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position    = "right"
  )

```

# Box Plot

```{r}

# Load & Prep Data
master <- read.csv("Master_Data_05_07_26.xlsx.csv")
master$Participant.ID <- sprintf("%04d", as.integer(master$Participant.ID))

all_data <- master[master$Participant.ID %in% c("0019", "0020", "0021", "0041", "0043",
                                                  "0002", "0003", "0004", "0009", "0014",
                                                  "0018", "0022", "0023", "0025", "0026",
                                                  "0029", "0030", "0031", "0035", "0042"), ]

# Recode stages
all_data$Stage[all_data$Stage %in% c("Mild Stage 3", "Moderate Stage 3")] <- "Stage 3"
all_data$Stage[all_data$Participant.ID %in% c("0019", "0020", "0021", "0041", "0043")] <- "Control"

# Clean and filter
all_data$Wear.Time    <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(all_data$Wear.Time))
all_data$WASO.minutes <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(all_data$WASO.minutes))
all_data$Night        <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(all_data$Night))

all_data <- all_data[!is.na(all_data$Wear.Time)    & all_data$Wear.Time    >= 600, ]
all_data <- all_data[!is.na(all_data$WASO.minutes) & !is.na(all_data$Night), ]
all_data <- all_data[all_data$Night >= 1 & all_data$Night <= 15, ]

#Collapse to participant-level averages 
participant_avg <- all_data %>%
  group_by(Participant.ID, Stage) %>%
  summarise(mean_waso = mean(WASO.minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

participant_avg$Stage <- factor(participant_avg$Stage,
                                levels = c("Control", "Stage 2", "Stage 3"))

#Colours 
group_colors <- c("Control" = "darkgreen",
                  "Stage 2" = "navy",
                  "Stage 3" = "red")

# Plot 
ggplot(participant_avg, aes(x = Stage, y = mean_waso, color = Stage)) +

  # Jittered points — one per participant
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.12,
    size  = 4,
    alpha = 0.7,
    shape = 16
  ) +

  # Box plot — unfilled so points show through
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.shape = NA,
    fill          = NA,
    linewidth     = 0.9,
    width         = 0.5
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 200, by = 50),
    limits = c(0, 210)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "WASO Box Plot by HD Stage",
    x     = "HD Stage",
    y     = "Wake After Sleep Onset (Minutes)"
  ) +

  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.line          = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.6),
    axis.ticks         = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.6),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    text               = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title         = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(size = 13),
    legend.position    = "none"
  )
```

```{r}
#T-Test Analysis for average (participant level) WASO between HD 2 and 3 vs. Control

# Calculate participant-level average WASO first (correct statistical approach)
hd_participant_avg <- waso_data %>%
  group_by(SUBJECT, STAGE) %>%
  summarise(mean_waso = mean(WASO, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ctrl_participant_avg <- ctrl_data %>%
  group_by(SUBJECT, STAGE) %>%
  summarise(mean_waso = mean(WASO, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Extract vectors for each group
stage2_waso  <- hd_participant_avg %>% filter(STAGE == "Stage 2") %>% pull(mean_waso)
stage3_waso  <- hd_participant_avg %>% filter(STAGE == "Stage 3") %>% pull(mean_waso)
control_waso <- ctrl_participant_avg %>% pull(mean_waso)

# ── Welch's t-tests (does not assume equal variance — safer for unequal group sizes)
ttest_s2_vs_ctrl <- t.test(stage2_waso, control_waso, var.equal = FALSE)
ttest_s3_vs_ctrl <- t.test(stage3_waso, control_waso, var.equal = FALSE)

# summary table
ttest_summary <- tibble(
  Comparison       = c("Stage 2 vs Control", "Stage 3 vs Control"),
  n_group1         = c(length(stage2_waso),  length(stage3_waso)),
  n_control        = c(length(control_waso), length(control_waso)),
  mean_group1      = c(mean(stage2_waso),    mean(stage3_waso)),
  mean_control     = c(mean(control_waso),   mean(control_waso)),
  mean_difference  = c(mean(stage2_waso) - mean(control_waso),
                       mean(stage3_waso) - mean(control_waso)),
  t_statistic      = c(ttest_s2_vs_ctrl$statistic, ttest_s3_vs_ctrl$statistic),
  df               = c(ttest_s2_vs_ctrl$parameter, ttest_s3_vs_ctrl$parameter),
  p_value          = c(ttest_s2_vs_ctrl$p.value,   ttest_s3_vs_ctrl$p.value),
  significant_0.05 = c(ttest_s2_vs_ctrl$p.value < 0.05,
                       ttest_s3_vs_ctrl$p.value < 0.05)
)

ttest_summary


# Descriptive Statistics (for reference)

all_participant_avg <- bind_rows(hd_participant_avg, ctrl_participant_avg)

group_stats <- all_participant_avg %>%
  group_by(STAGE) %>%
  summarise(
    n_participants    = n(),
    grand_mean_waso   = mean(mean_waso,   na.rm = TRUE),
    grand_sd_waso     = sd(mean_waso,     na.rm = TRUE),
    grand_median_waso = median(mean_waso, na.rm = TRUE),
    grand_min_waso    = min(mean_waso,    na.rm = TRUE),
    grand_max_waso    = max(mean_waso,    na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

# T-test Comparing Week 1 and Week 2 Averages

```{r}
# Week 1 vs Week 2 WASO T-tests (test-retest reliability)

# Step 1: Load and prep all three groups from master file
master <- read.csv("Master_Data_05_07_26.xlsx.csv")
master$Participant.ID <- sprintf("%04d", as.integer(master$Participant.ID))

# Combine all participants into one cleaned dataframe
all_data <- master[master$Participant.ID %in% c("0019", "0020", "0021", "0041", "0043",
                                                  "0002", "0003", "0004", "0009", "0014",
                                                  "0018", "0022", "0023", "0025", "0026",
                                                  "0029", "0030", "0031", "0035", "0042"), ]

# Recode stage
all_data$Stage[all_data$Stage %in% c("Mild Stage 3", "Moderate Stage 3")] <- "Stage 3"
all_data$Stage[all_data$Participant.ID %in% c("0019", "0020", "0021", "0041", "0043")] <- "Control"

# Clean wear time and filter
all_data$Wear.Time     <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(all_data$Wear.Time))
all_data$WASO.minutes  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(all_data$WASO.minutes))
all_data <- all_data[!is.na(all_data$Wear.Time)    & all_data$Wear.Time    >= 600, ]
all_data <- all_data[!is.na(all_data$WASO.minutes) & !is.na(all_data$Night), ]
all_data <- all_data[all_data$Night >= 1 & all_data$Night <= 15, ]

# Step 2: Assign week
all_data$Week <- ifelse(all_data$Night <= 7, "Week1", "Week2")

# Step 3: Participant-level average WASO per week
participant_week_avg <- aggregate(
  WASO.minutes ~ Participant.ID + Stage + Week,
  data = all_data,
  FUN  = mean
)

# Step 4: Reshape to wide format so each participant has one row
#         with a Week1 and Week2 column
install.packages("reshape2")
library(reshape2)
participant_wide <- dcast(participant_week_avg,
                          Participant.ID + Stage ~ Week,
                          value.var = "WASO.minutes")

# Remove participants missing either week (can't run paired test without both)
participant_wide <- participant_wide[!is.na(participant_wide$Week1) &
                                     !is.na(participant_wide$Week2), ]


# Step 5: Run paired t-tests for each group
run_paired_ttest <- function(data, group_name) {
  grp <- data[data$Stage == group_name, ]
  
  cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat(" Group:", group_name, "| n =", nrow(grp), "\n")
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  
  if (nrow(grp) < 2) {
    cat("  Not enough participants to run t-test (n < 2)\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("  Week 1 mean WASO: %.2f minutes\n", mean(grp$Week1)))
  cat(sprintf("  Week 2 mean WASO: %.2f minutes\n", mean(grp$Week2)))
  cat(sprintf("  Mean difference (W2 - W1): %.2f minutes\n",
              mean(grp$Week2 - grp$Week1)))
  cat("\n")
  
  result <- t.test(grp$Week1, grp$Week2, paired = TRUE)
  print(result)
  return(result)
}

ttest_stage2  <- run_paired_ttest(participant_wide, "Stage 2")
ttest_stage3  <- run_paired_ttest(participant_wide, "Stage 3")
ttest_control <- run_paired_ttest(participant_wide, "Control")


# Step 6: Clean summary table
library(knitr)

make_row <- function(data, group_name, result) {
  grp <- data[data$Stage == group_name, ]
  if (is.null(result)) return(NULL)
  data.frame(
    Group           = group_name,
    n               = nrow(grp),
    Week1_Mean_WASO = round(mean(grp$Week1), 2),
    Week2_Mean_WASO = round(mean(grp$Week2), 2),
    Mean_Difference = round(mean(grp$Week2 - grp$Week1), 2),
    t_statistic     = round(result$statistic, 3),
    df              = round(result$parameter, 2),
    p_value         = round(result$p.value, 4),
    Significance    = ifelse(result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns")
  )
}

summary_table <- rbind(
  make_row(participant_wide, "Stage 2",  ttest_stage2),
  make_row(participant_wide, "Stage 3",  ttest_stage3),
  make_row(participant_wide, "Control",  ttest_control)
)

cat("\n── Week 1 vs Week 2 Paired T-test Summary ───────────────\n\n")
print(knitr::kable(summary_table,
                   col.names = c("Group", "n", "Week 1 Mean WASO",
                                 "Week 2 Mean WASO", "Mean Diff (W2-W1)",
                                 "t", "df", "p value", "Sig"),
                   align = "lcccccccc"))

write.csv(summary_table, "Week1_vs_Week2_WASO_ttest.csv", row.names = FALSE)
participant_wide
```
